description: Migration: creates the per-client visibility groups table with tenant and client ownership fields.
Migration: creates the group-to-board membership table with tenant-scoped references.
Migration: adds the single-group contact assignment field or equivalent structure with `NULL` representing unrestricted access.
Migration/backward compatibility: existing contacts and portal users remain unrestricted when no group assignment exists.
Integration: effective board resolver returns unrestricted access when a contact has no assigned group.
Integration: effective board resolver returns exactly the assigned group's allowed board IDs for a restricted contact.
Guard: effective board resolver rejects or ignores groups that belong to a different client or tenant.
Integration: client portal ticket list only returns tickets from boards in the assigned visibility group.
Regression: client portal ticket list still returns all client tickets for an unassigned contact.
Integration: client portal ticket detail succeeds for a ticket on a visible board.
Guard: client portal ticket detail rejects direct access to a ticket on a hidden board.
Guard: client portal ticket documents or comments cannot be loaded for a ticket on a hidden board.
Integration: client portal ticket creation form only lists allowed boards for a restricted contact.
Integration: client portal ticket creation default board selection stays within the allowed board set.
Guard: client portal ticket creation rejects a disallowed board even if the board ID is submitted manually.
Regression: unassigned contacts can still create tickets using the current unrestricted behavior.
Integration: ticket-backed dashboard counts or summaries only include boards visible to the current restricted contact.
UI/action: client portal admin can list visibility groups for their own client.
Guard: non-admin client portal users are denied access to visibility group listing actions and UI.
Guard: client portal admins cannot list or fetch visibility groups for a different client.
UI/action: client portal admin can create a visibility group with selected board membership.
UI/action: client portal admin can edit a group's name and board membership.
Guard: client portal admin cannot add boards from another client or tenant to a visibility group.
UI/action: client portal admin can assign a visibility group to a contact in the same client.
UI/action: client portal admin can clear a contact's assignment back to unrestricted full access.
Guard: client portal admin cannot assign a group from one client to a contact in another client.
Lifecycle: a contact without a portal user can receive a visibility group assignment before invitation or account creation.
Lifecycle: a preconfigured contact assignment is still effective after the portal user account is created and linked to that contact.
UI/action: MSP staff can view and replace the effective visibility group from the PSA contact portal tab.
UI/action: MSP staff can create and edit per-client visibility groups from the PSA-side management flow.
Integration: when MSP staff replace a contact assignment, the new group is immediately reflected in client portal ticket visibility.
Integration: when a client portal admin changes the same contact assignment later, the latest assignment wins without lock semantics.
Guard: deleting a group that is currently assigned to one or more contacts is blocked with a clear error response.
Happy path: deleting an unassigned visibility group succeeds.
Localization: English client portal locale contains every new board-visibility group key used by the client portal admin UI.
Localization parity: `de`, `es`, `fr`, `it`, `nl`, `pl`, `xx`, and `yy` client portal locale files contain the same new board-visibility keys as `en`.
UI: an assigned group with zero boards yields a localized empty state and hides or disables ticket creation for the restricted portal user.
Regression: existing portal invitation and client portal onboarding flows still work when no board visibility group is assigned.
